macro_summary: US equity futures are softer this Monday morning — S&P futures -0.28%, Nasdaq futures -0.32%, Dow futures -0.17% — as geopolitical risk re-emerges on the first day of US-Iran talks: Trump threatened fresh strikes and Tehran briefly suspended negotiations, driving crude oil up ~1.45% to ~$77/barrel. The dominant macro backdrop remains a hawkish Federal Reserve: Chair Kevin Warsh's inaugural June 17 FOMC meeting produced a unanimous hold at 3.5–3.75%, but the dot plot now shows 9 of 18 officials penciling in at least one rate hike by year-end, with markets pricing the first move as early as October. This week is data-dense and event-rich: Carnival (CCL) and FedEx (FDX) report Tuesday, Micron (MU) and Fed bank stress test results land Wednesday, and May PCE — the Fed's preferred inflation gauge — prints Thursday. Today itself is quiet on scheduled data, making it a positioning day ahead of a heavy mid-week.
key_events: US-Iran talks begin Monday; Trump threatened strikes, Tehran briefly suspended talks — crude oil +1.45% to ~$77/bbl, futures lower
Fed (June 17): Held rates at 3.5–3.75% unanimously; dot plot shifts hawkish — 9/18 officials see at least one hike in 2026; median year-end FFR projection 3.8%; market pricing Oct hike
INTC surged ~10.5% Thursday after Trump Truth Social post claiming Apple-Intel US chip deal; deal remains preliminary/unconfirmed by both companies
Micron (MU) reports fiscal Q3 2026 earnings Wednesday June 24 after close — consensus ~$34.7B revenue / ~$19.95 EPS
Fed 2026 bank stress test results for 32 large banks due Wednesday June 24 at 4pm ET — Fed confirmed results will NOT affect 2026 capital requirements
May PCE price index (Fed's preferred inflation gauge) releases Thursday June 25 — April PCE was 3.77% YoY headline, 3.29% core; prior core PCE 3.3% in April vs 2% Fed target
Q1 GDP final estimate and May durable goods orders also due Thursday June 25

ideas.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Micron reports fiscal Q3 2026 earnings Wednesday June 24 after close, with Wall Street unanimously bullish and HBM memory capacity described as fully sold out. The company's Q2 print already crushed consensus by ~42% (reported $12.20 EPS vs $8.60 expected), and management guided Q3 to record revenue of $33.5B ±$0.75B with ~81% gross margin — a figure the street has now exceeded to ~$34.7B. The AI-driven demand for High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) is structurally different from prior DRAM cycles, with UBS noting long-term volume and pricing agreements locked in with major hyperscaler customers.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings report Wednesday June 24, 2026 after market close — with Q4 guidance and HBM4 allocation commentary as the key swing factors for the stock
ideas.edge: Stock trades at ~17x forward earnings despite ~300% YTD gain; prior quarter beat consensus by ~42%; company guided Q3 EPS to ~$19.15 midpoint vs street at ~$19.95 — street already above guide, leaving room for further upside surprise. Full-year FY2026 EPS consensus ~$57.71, up ~651% YoY. Deutsche Bank and TD Cowen targets at $1,500; Morgan Stanley at $1,050.
ideas.bull_case: A third consecutive massive earnings beat plus strong Q4 guidance — especially on HBM4 volume and margin sustainability above 81% — could drive another leg higher. Long-term pricing agreements (LTAs) are transforming MU's business model from cyclical to secular growth, supporting a durable re-rating.
ideas.bear_case: Expectations are now extremely elevated after a ~300% YTD run; any miss on Q4 revenue guidance, margin compression hint, or commentary suggesting hyperscaler AI capex pullback could trigger a sharp selloff. SK Hynix holds ~62% HBM market share and is ahead of Micron on per-pin speed in HBM4.

ideas.name: Intel Corporation
ideas.thesis: Intel has accumulated a rare cluster of catalysts in one week: Trump's June 18 Truth Social post claiming Apple agreed to work with Intel to build chips in the US, and Intel's own announcement that its 18A-P node (promising 9% higher performance or 18% lower power vs. 18A) entered risk production at its Arizona fab. Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has guided for multiple foundry customer commitments in the second half of 2026. The Apple arrangement, if formalized, would validate Intel's foundry credibility to other potential customers and support a continued re-rating.
ideas.catalyst: Expected formal foundry customer commitments from Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan in H2 2026; 18A-P node qualification milestones; any official Apple or Intel SEC disclosure confirming the manufacturing deal
ideas.edge: Stock surged ~464% in the past 12 months and ~200%+ YTD, with the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index up ~90% YTD; the US government holds a ~10% equity stake (valued at ~$60B+). However, both Apple and Intel have declined to formally confirm the deal, meaning the quantifiable upside from here depends on contract execution rather than announcement. Risk/reward is less clear post-pop — flagged as medium accordingly.
ideas.bull_case: A formal Apple foundry commitment in H2 2026 would be the first major outside customer for Intel's advanced nodes, validating the 18A/18A-P platform and unlocking additional Nvidia, SpaceX, and other hyperscaler relationships the Trump administration is reportedly encouraging.
ideas.bear_case: The deal remains 'preliminary' with no confirmed devices, volumes, or timeline; Apple analyst Ben Bajarin noted Apple will likely wait for 18A-P and that Intel 'has not done' Arm chip manufacturing — a major technical gap vs. TSMC. A failure to land signed contracts by year-end could reverse the stock's premium valuation.

ideas.name: Carnival Corporation & plc
ideas.thesis: Carnival reports fiscal Q2 2026 earnings Tuesday June 23, coming off a record Q1 where it beat estimates, raised full-year guidance, announced a $2.5B share buyback, and introduced its 'PROPEL' long-term targets through 2029. Q1 net yields hit record highs and 2026 bookings are up double digits at historically high prices. Oil prices falling from their Iran-war spike peak (Strait of Hormuz reopened via interim peace deal) reduces a key headwind for cruise fuel costs. The setup is a strong fundamental momentum story into a near-term earnings print.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q2 2026 earnings release Tuesday June 23, 2026 — with forward bookings commentary, yield guidance for H2 2026, and fuel cost update as key swing variables
ideas.edge: Company targets $7B in adjusted EBITDA for full-year 2026; Q1 EPS was $0.20, up 50% YoY, beating guidance on strong close-in demand; the newly announced $2.5B buyback provides a floor. Declining oil prices vs. Q1 fuel cost assumptions create potential for a guidance raise on fuel savings. No specific analyst price-target gap confirmed — flagged as medium.
ideas.bull_case: A Q2 beat-and-raise fueled by continued record bookings, easing fuel costs post-Iran peace deal, and strong close-in demand could re-accelerate the stock and validate the PROPEL 2029 growth framework.
ideas.bear_case: Consumer spending durability is uncertain in a hawkish rate environment where the Fed is signaling possible hikes; elevated inflation (CPI 4.17% YoY in May) pressures discretionary travel spending. Any softening of forward booking commentary would disappoint given elevated expectations.
